trajectory_summary: This transcript does not contain a call. The rep reached a voicemail at 00:01, left no message, and the remaining audio (02:13–02:50) is ambient customer speech directed at a third party — likely the customer's own phone recording or a crossed line. There is no rep-customer interaction to analyze, no qualification gathered, no appointment set, and no energy arc of any kind.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is listed as 'unknown.' That is accurate. There is no bookable outcome, no conversation, and no lead progression. The correct classification is 'no contact — voicemail reached, no message left.' If Ion's CRM logged this as any form of contact or attempt, that tag should be corrected to reflect the absence of a live interaction.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Voicemail policy and CRM disposition discipline
primary_coaching_focus.quote: Please leave a message after the tone.
primary_coaching_focus.why: The only actionable coaching question this call raises is whether the rep's silence after the voicemail prompt is compliant with floor policy or a missed touch. Manager needs to (1) confirm Ion's voicemail policy, (2) ensure the rep has a standard voicemail script if one is required, and (3) verify the CRM disposition on this call accurately reflects 'no live contact' so the lead gets a proper follow-up attempt. If this is a paid lead, the bill-collection gate means this lead is stalled until a live connect is made — the follow-up cadence matters.

key_moments.quote: The Google subscriber you have called is not available.
key_moments.stewart_read: The rep reached voicemail and left no message. Two competing reads: (a) Reactive — the rep hung up without leaving a callback message, losing the only touch point on this attempt. (b) Strategic — Ion's floor may have a 'no voicemail' policy to avoid tipping off leads before a live connect. The transcript cannot resolve this. The reactive read is the default until floor policy is confirmed. The alternative hypothesis is not dismissed — it is genuinely unresolved.

key_moments.quote: Please leave a message after the tone.
key_moments.stewart_read: No rep speech follows this prompt anywhere in the transcript. The absence of a message is the only observable rep behavior on this call. If this is a paid lead, the bill is required before the appointment can be set, making a voicemail callback message the only path to lead progression from this attempt. If organic or recycled, the urgency is lower. Manager should confirm lead type before routing this coaching note.

This customer utterance is ambient audio — the customer is speaking to a third party, not the rep. It is not analyzable as a sales interaction. Its presence in the transcript suggests a recording artifact: either the call was not fully disconnected after voicemail, or the customer's line was open and capturing background conversation. No coaching signal can be drawn from this content.

key_moments.quote: Are we doing drag or street?
key_moments.stewart_read: Customer is mid-conversation with a third party about an unrelated topic. This confirms the post-00:04 audio is entirely ambient and contains no setter-customer interaction. The transcript as a whole does not constitute a call that can be analyzed for sales technique, qualification, or appointment-setting.
